underlying_goal: Investigate what reasoning/thinking content is observable in Claude Code session logs across different contexts (parent sessions, subagents, headless invocations) and document findings in the measurement signal inventory
friction_detail: Claude initially gave shallow conclusions without rigorous testing of alternatives, and later massively over-interpreted 'think for a bit' as a writing task instead of a simple delegation experiment
brief_summary: User wanted to investigate thinking-token observability across session contexts; after early shallow analysis was challenged, Claude ran a rigorous spike that falsified assumptions, discovered subagents emit zero thinking blocks, verified headless CLI bypasses the gate, and documented findings.
